latestCommit.message: Remove .png and .pth files from repository tracking
Temp : Upload & Analysis video API function works
Make 3-illegal auto-skip a turn rotation, not a pass

Fix TimeScrubber swallowing shortcut keys when slider has focus

Range / checkbox / button inputs never produce text, so the early-return
that protects text-typing inputs was wrong to include them. After clicking
the slider, =/-/+/_/r etc were going to the input and getting eaten.

Add GPU release/acquire scripts with CPU fallback and focus restore

The voice daemon used to keep its CUDA model resident in VRAM
indefinitely, blocking other workloads (training, image gen, etc.)
from using the GPU. The previous timer-based auto-unload tried to
solve this but interrupted 秒按秒錄 latency every few minutes and
could deadlock the transcribe path under GPU contention.

Replace it with two explicit user-driven scripts and an in-daemon
primary/fallback model machinery:

* scripts/release-gpu.{ps1,sh} writes a sentinel file. A background
  watcher thread in the daemon sees the sentinel within ~1s, unloads
  the CUDA primary from the GPU, lazy-loads a CPU small model, and
  switches active transcription to it. Voice keeps working — just a
  few seconds slower per utterance.

* scripts/acquire-gpu.{ps1,sh} removes the sentinel. The watcher
  reloads the primary onto the GPU (~1-2s warm) and switches back.

* Both transitions are user-driven; the daemon never auto-acquires
  because it has no way to tell whether the other workload is done.

* If the daemon is launched (by SessionStart / preexec) while the
  sentinel is already present, primary is skipped entirely and the
  daemon boots straight into fallback — no VRAM spike.

* CPU-only systems (Mac without CUDA) ignore the sentinel since
  there's no GPU to release.

Also fixes a separate UX bug that became more noticeable with CPU
fallback's longer transcription delay: the window that had focus
when the user pressed the hotkey is now snapshotted at recording
start and restored before Ctrl+V / Cmd+V, so the paste lands in
the right window even if the user switched apps mid-transcription.
Windows uses SetForegroundWindow + AttachThreadInput, macOS uses
NSRunningApplication.activateWithOptions:, Linux uses xdotool
windowactivate --sync.

Removes the now-superseded VOICE_IDLE_UNLOAD_SEC env. Adds
VOICE_FALLBACK_MODEL_SIZE and VOICE_FALLBACK_COMPUTE_TYPE for
tuning the fallback model. Both READMEs, the install scripts'
default .env template, and .env are updated accordingly.

Move ApkSmith submodule from droidbot/ to project root

The submodule was unnecessarily nested at droidbot/ApkSmith/ — it is
a project-level dependency, not specific to the droidbot subdirectory.

Moved to SADroid/ApkSmith/ and replaced the sys.path hacks in both
driver files with clean imports:

  # Before (fragile, path-dependent)
  sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(os.path.dirname(__file__), 'ApkSmith', 'src'))
  from apksmith import instrument_apk  # noqa: E402

  # After (standard pip package)
  from apksmith import instrument_apk

Setup: run `pip install -e ApkSmith/` from the SADroid root to make
the apksmith package available as a normal import.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix all ruff lint errors + stabilise E2E test

Lint fixes (38 issues across 11 files):
- Import sorting (I001) via ruff --fix
- Line too long (E501): break strings, reflow args
- Lambda assignments (E731): convert to def functions
- Unnecessary f-strings (F541): remove extraneous f prefix
- Unused imports (F401): remove dead imports
- Nested with statements (SIM117): combine into single with
- Unnecessary mode arg (UP015): drop "r" from open()

E2E test stability fixes:
- Add force-stop before launching so onCreate always re-executes
- Enlarge logcat buffer to 4M and add 1s settle time after clear
- Replace logcat tag-based filtering (unreliable on some emulator
  images) with full drain + Python-side grep
- All 40 tests pass (39 unit + 1 E2E), CI lint clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
